description:
You are a forensic anthropologist conducting a skeletal identification case. An unidentified set of remains was recovered and you must complete a full craniometric assessment from CT imaging to estimate the biological profile (sex, ancestry, stature potential) and document the skull morphology for the forensic record. A CT imaging series is already loaded in InVesalius 3. A forensic case brief is at /home/ga/Documents/forensic_case/case_brief.txt.

Complete a forensic craniometric protocol including at least 10 linear measurements following standard practice. Your protocol must include bilateral measurements (corresponding structures on both the left and right sides of the skull) and overall cranial dimensions: maximum cranial length (glabella to opisthocranion), maximum cranial breadth (biparietal), and cranial height (basion to bregma). Export the 3D bone surface as a binary STL file to /home/ga/Documents/forensic_case/skull_surface.stl. Save screenshots of the 3D surface in Norma frontalis, Norma lateralis, and Norma verticalis orientations to /home/ga/Documents/forensic_case/norma_frontalis.png, /home/ga/Documents/forensic_case/norma_lateralis.png, and /home/ga/Documents/forensic_case/norma_verticalis.png respectively. Save the complete InVesalius project to /home/ga/Documents/forensic_case/forensic_analysis.inv3.

Login credentials: username=ga, password=password123
